title: Post-Production Audit Decision Replay Packet
description: Immutable record of audit inputs, environment state, and decision logic for replay-auditing and cross-run comparison. Enables verification that audit decisions are reproducible and based on documented assumptions.

audit_slug.description: Unique identifier for this audit run

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when audit was executed

window_start.description: Inclusive start timestamp for the replay evidence window

window_end.description: Inclusive end timestamp for the replay evidence window

trigger_category.description: Which trigger category caused this audit to run

query_hashes.description: SHA-256 hashes of all queries used in source-of-truth verification

schema_version.description: Version of the source-of-truth schema at audit time (e.g., '1.2.3')

trigger_contract_version.description: Version of the Trigger Contract in effect when this audit was run

bulk_mutation_threshold.description: Record count threshold used in this audit (copy of {MANUAL:BULK_MUTATION_THRESHOLD})

duplicate_cluster_cap.description: Maximum duplicate clusters to sample (copy of {MANUAL:DUPLICATE_CLUSTER_CAP})

sampling_tier.description: Risk tier used to select sampling rate

sampling_criteria.description: Selection logic applied (deterministic ordering rules, inclusion predicates)

environment_metadata.description: State of tools and infrastructure at audit time

tool_versions.description: Versions of key tools used (e.g., database client, API client)

database_system.description: Database or system being audited (e.g., 'PostgreSQL 14.5', 'API v2.3')

consistency_window_seconds.description: If eventual-consistent system, max lag window in seconds

platform_drift_detected.description: Whether schema/API changes were detected since last audit

verdict_inputs.description: Key metrics that influenced the verdict decision

defect_rate_estimate.description: Estimated defect rate as decimal (e.g., 0.03 for 3%)

unknown_rate.description: Percentage of sampled rows with UNKNOWN result

critical_defect_found.description: Whether any critical defect (data loss, wrong mutation) was found

evidence_link_manifest.description: List of evidence artifacts referenced in audit findings

file_path.description: Path to evidence file relative to audit directory

query_hash.description: If evidence is query output, SHA-256 of the query

crossrun_comparison_enabled.description: Whether this packet was prepared for cross-run metric comparison

agent_session_trajectory.description: Phase 1 extension. Ordered record of agent-to-agent handoff edges within a single generated-team session. Present only on packets that capture an agent-team session (optional, so audit-decision-only packets still validate). Walking handoff_edges by ascending sequence and following from_agent -> to_agent reconstructs multi-step handoff chains across component-experts.

session_slug.description: Identifier for the agent-team session this trajectory belongs to

root_agent.description: Slug of the agent that initiated the session (typically 'orchestrator')

handoff_edges.description: Directed handoff edges in invocation order. The multi-step chain is recoverable by sorting on `sequence` and chaining edges where one edge's to_agent is the next edge's from_agent.

sequence.description: Monotonic 0-based ordering index of this handoff within the session

from_agent.description: Slug of the agent handing off control

to_agent.description: Slug of the agent receiving control

payload_schema_id.description: Optional $id of the typed handoff payload schema carried across this edge. Compared $id-only, consistent with agentteams/handoff_payloads.audit_handoff_chain (Cluster C payload_schema_in/out substrate). Absent for untyped handoffs.

mediated_by.description: Optional slug of the governance agent (e.g. 'orchestrator') that mediated this edge when coordination is not peer-to-peer. Lets a trajectory record orchestrator-mediated worker handoffs without collapsing the logical from_agent -> to_agent chain.
